title: Buildprint Mapper OS
creator: Agent Buildprint
summary: Agent-session workflow for mapping an existing repo into an executable, source-independent, typed Buildprint packet that improves downstream artifact quality through mode-aware setup, usable phase slices, skeptical review, and concise handover.
plainDescription: Use Mapper OS when you have a working repo and want an agent to turn it into a clean, reusable Buildprint: map the source, classify the artifact type, choose a scoped candidate, then extract a selected-buildprint packet that a fresh coding agent can implement with product/developer/operator judgment, phase-flow, honest boundaries, final review, and handover without reading the original code.
promise: A repo-to-Buildprint workflow that preserves scope fidelity, classifies artifact type, generates typed executable phase files, and validates packet structure without encouraging proof theater.
whatYouGet: A selected-buildprint/ executable packet with BUILDPRINT.md, 01-questions.md, 02-project-setup.md, blueprint.yaml, phase-index.yaml, phase-flow.md, phase files, 04-review.md, and 05-handover.md

Typed setup that names artifact mode, real consumer, central artifact/interface/boundary, first usable loop, persistence/traces/readback, boundaries, fake-feel risks, local commands, and forbidden shortcuts
Phase-flow execution contract that asks for a real usable artifact-type slice, the obvious next consumer action, relevant local checks, and visible slop removal
Final artifact review for core loop, reload/restart/rerun, input/config/event variation, controls/commands/API calls/operator actions, empty/error/blocked states, and fake/generic behavior
Concise handover with status, built surfaces, verification, known defects/blockers, and next atomic actions
whatYouNeed: GitHub/private repo access if the source is private
A human scope choice when multiple candidates exist
Permission to use any private source code
includes: BUILDPRINT.md execution start and read order

02-project-setup.md artifact mode, consumer, first loop, central artifact/interface/boundary, persistence/traces/readback, boundaries, fake-feel risks, commands, quality rules, and forbidden shortcuts
Mode-aware phase files with intention, Build, Quality bar, and Do not ship sections
risks: Packet overfits source internals instead of observable behavior
Generated phases become generic dashboard work
Agent writes proof-looking prose instead of building a usable loop
Provider/deployment/security boundaries are hidden instead of named
Non-product artifacts are forced into product-app UI phases
checks: Selected output uses executable Buildprint with BUILDPRINT.md, 01-questions.md, 02-project-setup.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/phase-index.yaml, 03-phases/phase-flow.md, phase files, 04-review.md, and 05-handover.md
blueprint.yaml declares execution_start, machine_contract, qualification/status label, setup_tier, blueprint_mode, implementation_loop, and repair_routing
Phase files contain mode-appropriate intention, build scope, quality bar, and do-not-ship failures
Generated prompt is alignment speech, not authority
Packet avoids obsolete routers, evidence-ledger bureaucracy, packet AGENTS.md, and fragmented mini-files
Review/handover path names local checks, manual/browser/API/CLI/operator review when relevant, blockers, and next atomic actions

trustBadges.label: Typed extraction
trustBadges.detail: Selected packets declare blueprint_mode so product apps, integrations, frameworks, services, automations, pipelines, and infrastructure get different phase language.

trustBadges.label: Phase-flow replay
trustBadges.detail: Generated packets contain phase-flow.md and per-phase implementation grammar so a fresh agent can execute bounded slices.

trustBadges.label: Fresh-agent isolation
trustBadges.detail: Selected packets must be enough for an isolated downstream agent without original source access or parent-directory scans.

trustBadges.label: No fake scans
trustBadges.detail: Verification claims require actual command/artifact evidence; blockers and synthetic checks cannot upgrade readiness claims.

howToUse.title: 1. Point it at a repo
howToUse.detail: Open the source repo in your coding agent, or give the agent a repo URL/local path. If the target is unclear, Mapper OS must ask exactly: Which repo URL or local path should I map?

howToUse.title: 2. Bootstrap the Mapper OS package
howToUse.detail: Copy the agent prompt from this page, or run agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json and follow .buildprint/next-agent.md. Do not use an agb map command.

howToUse.title: 3. Let it discover, not extract yet
howToUse.detail: The agent should inspect the repo and produce SYSTEM_MAP.md plus BUILDPRINT_CANDIDATES.md. For broad repos, this is where it stops and asks you which candidate to extract.

howToUse.title: 4. Choose the scope
howToUse.detail: Pick one product, feature, workflow, integration, framework, service, automation, dataflow, or operation. Smaller is fine; vague whole-repo sludge is not. Full-system mode should be explicit.

howToUse.title: 5. Extract the executable packet
howToUse.detail: The selected package should be an executable typed Buildprint with BUILDPRINT.md, blueprint.yaml including blueprint_mode, setup requirements, phase files, review gates, and concise handover.

resultChecklist: Selected scope and source-independent artifact promise
Artifact mode, real consumer, central artifact/interface/boundary, and first usable loop
Phase-flow and phase files for usable implementation slices
Mode-appropriate handling for UI, API/library, integration, automation, data-pipeline, and infrastructure surfaces
Honest blockers for credentials, deployment, destructive actions, paid services, and security review
copyPrompt: Use the Buildprint Mapper OS Buildprint to map a target repo in the current agent session. First bootstrap exact snapshots: agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json . If agb is not installed, clone https://github.com/DomEscobar/agent-buildprint and run node agent-buildprint/bin/agb.js start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json . Then read .buildprint/next-agent.md and continue. Do not write Buildprint snapshots manually. Before discovery, resolve the target: if no repository URL, local path, or already-opened working repo is given, ask exactly one blocking question — 'Which repo URL or local path should I map?' — and do not start until it is explicit.
